dispositions.note: The candidate now places the artifact in a system-reference namespace, indexes it only as a reference, forbids component/role/future-module registration, and stops for governance review if the manifest cannot represent the reference without a boundary or schema change.

The candidate now treats URLs and dates only as provenance metadata, requires exact locally reviewed evidence artifacts with content digests, pins the pre-review and TELOS examples with complete stable references, and rejects abbreviated or mutable terminal anchors.

The candidate now specifies a deterministic ESM renderer with write and byte-equality check modes, a generated-file notice, machine-record-only substantive content, and acceptance coverage through both the renderer check and local fail-closed checker.

The candidate now preserves the five-workflow-versus-agent distinction, qualifies TELOS mappings as evidence-backed related examples rather than proven instantiations, removes advisory-created mandatory checkpoint language, and limits the oracle to structural and provenance claims rather than semantic optimality or safety.
